summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 12: re-detect against main (73 bugs resolved/triaged rounds 0-4). Deepest probes into the least-covered surfaces (audit attribute filtering, realtime broadcast dispatch, mcp tools, cli generators, export xlsx/formatting, i18n/locale, theme, command palette, a11y, edge-case interactions between traits). Seen-filtered (79 sigs), adversarial verify. Stop after 2 consecutive clean rounds.
logs: Round 12 detection: 2 from clusters = 2 after dedup/seen-filter
Round 12 verify: 2 confirmed NEW framework bugs, 0 app-misuse, 0 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Audit activity-log read endpoints ship wired with web,auth only and ZERO controller authorization — any authenticated user reads the entire global audit log and any record's activity
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/audit/routes/admin.php:9-16 (routes wired with ['web','auth'] only); packages/audit/src/Http/Controllers/GlobalActivityLogController.php:35 (index — no Gate/Policy/abort); packages/audit/src/Http/Controllers/RecordActivityController.php:28 (show — no Gate/Policy/abort); enabled by packages/audit/src/AuditServiceProvider.php:30 (->hasRoute('admin'))
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Add controller-level authorization mirroring the framework convention: in GlobalActivityLogController::index gate on a global audit ability (e.g. abort_unless(Gate::allows('viewAny', Activity::class) || Gate::allows('view-audit-log'), 403)) and in RecordActivityController::show resolve the subject model and apply Gate/Policy 'view' honoring Gate::has + Gate::getPolicyFor (same deniesView() pattern as VersionHistoryController), defaulting to deny-when-gate/policy-exists; alternatively attach a configurable 'can:' middleware in routes/admin.php. If consumer-responsibility is truly intended, that must be documented with an explicit SECURITY NOTE like ExportDownloadController — currently it is not.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced structurally on current main (not in worktrees). packages/audit/routes/admin.php wires GET /admin/audit/activity (GlobalActivityLogController::index) and GET /admin/audit/{subjectType}/{subjectId}/activity (RecordActivityController::show) behind ['web','auth'] only. grep for gate|policy|abort|authorize|can: across packages/audit/src returned EMPTY — zero authorization anywhere. GlobalActivityLogController::index returns the full Activity::query() paginator (every user's causer name/email, subject_type/id, full property diffs) to any authenticated user. The AuditServiceProvider is auto-discovered (composer.json extra.laravel.providers) and now calls ->hasRoute('admin'), so the routes ship live; showcase has no app-level re-gating and Post uses LogsActivity (real entries exist). This diverges from the framework's OWN established convention: widgets/DashboardController::show does abort_unless($dashboard->canBeSeenBy(...),403) and versioning/VersionHistoryController::deniesView() honors Gate::has('view') AND Gate::getPolicyFor (the exact issue-#91 / rounds-5/6 fix). REFUTATION ATTEMPT: the only documented 'consumer-responsibility, web+auth-only, no controller authz' precedent is ExportDownloadController — but it carries a prominent SECURITY NOTE docblock AND the export design is consumer-must-wrap by spec. The audit package has NO security note in either controller (RecordActivityController's 'consumer responsibility' note is exclusively about slug->FQCN resolution, explicitly NOT authz), and SKILL.md Anti-patterns covers PII-in-fillable but says nothing about gating these read routes. AUDIT-003's own ticket spec intended an ActivityLogResource extends Resource (which flows through ResourceController::authorize Policy/Gate enforcement); the implementer deferred that Resource integration and shipped raw ungated JSON endpoints instead, silently dropping the authorization layer the ticket's 'filter por user' criterion assumed. So this is an undocumented divergence, not intended/deferred behavior. Could not refute. Distinct from the prior 'audit app-misuse' triage (that concerned LogsActivity attribute selection, not these now-wired read routes). Severity high: cross-user/cross-tenant audit data disclosure (who-did-what, causer email, full diffs) to any low-privilege logged-in account; framework-shipped and live. Not blocker because it requires authentication (login-gated) and impact is read-only information disclosure rather than data destruction or auth bypass.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/audit/src/AuditServiceProvider.php now registers `->hasRoute('admin')`, and packages/audit/routes/admin.php wires GET /admin/audit/activity (GlobalActivityLogController::index) and GET /admin/audit/{subjectType}/{subjectId}/activity (RecordActivityController::show) behind ONLY ['web','auth']. Neither controller performs ANY authorization (no Gate/Policy/abort) — GlobalActivityLogController::index returns the full Activity::query() paginator with causer/subject/properties; RecordActivityController::show accepts an attacker-controlled FQCN as subjectType (route ->where('subjectType','.*')) and returns that model's activity. This diverges from the framework's own established convention: versioning (VersionHistoryController, Gate::has()-vs-Policy fix rounds 5/6) and widgets (DashboardController::show abort_unless canBeSeenBy) pair route-level web,auth with controller-level Policy/Gate authorization. Live repro in apps/showcase (routes confirmed via `php artisan route:list | grep audit`): a freshly-created low-privilege User with no role/policy grant successfully GETs /admin/audit/activity and sees an admin-caused entry ('Admin changed a secret post', event 'updated'), AND GETs /admin/audit/{Post FQCN}/{id}/activity returning non-empty data. Test passed (2 tests, 5 assertions) then removed; full showcase suite green (8 passed). This exposes who-did-what across ALL users/subjects (cross-tenant audit data: causer name/email, subject_type/id, full properties diff) to any logged-in account. The audit endpoints were previously route-less (provider comment said 'Routes ... intentionally NOT registered'), so this is a distinct, newly-live entry point, not a re-report of the prior 'audit app-misuse' triage which concerned LogsActivity attribute selection, not these now-wired read routes.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Table Column::canSee() / Filter::canSee() per-record visibility predicates are dead — never consulted at serialize or query-apply time
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/core/src/Support/InertiaDataBuilder.php:530 (serializeRecord — no per-column Column::isVisibleFor redaction; columns/filters serialized via serializeMany at 205-206 ignore canSee) and packages/table/src/TableQueryBuilder.php:80-87 (applyFilters applies every filter without Filter::isVisibleFor check)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Serialize path: in InertiaDataBuilder::serializeRecord, when building the row payload, unset cells whose Column::isVisibleFor($record) returns false (requires threading the table's columns + user into serializeRecord; today it only has the record). Apply path: in TableQueryBuilder::applyFilters, skip `$filter->applyToQuery(...)` when `$filter->isVisibleFor()` is false (requires threading the authenticated user into TableQueryBuilder so the canSee closure can authorize). Also fix/qualify the Table.php:25-27 docblock which currently falsely promises canSee is honoured at serialize time.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced on current main. grep confirms Column::isVisibleFor/getCanSeeCallback and Filter::isVisibleFor have ZERO callers in packages/table/src and packages/core/src. The only isVisibleFor calls in InertiaDataBuilder (lines 591/652 via resolveVisibleActionNames at 639) are for Action objects, not Column/Filter — so per-record visibility is wired for Actions but NOT for Columns/Filters, contradicting round-6's assertion that Column/Filter were already wired. Serialize path: serializeMany(getColumns)/serializeMany(getFilters) (InertiaDataBuilder.php:205-206) routes through callToArray which only ever calls toArray()/toArray($user); Column::toArray() (Column.php:360) emits only the static `hidden` bool, never per-record canSee. serializeRecord (530-557) dumps the entire $record->toArray() with no cell-level redaction, so a column gated by ->canSee(fn($r)=>...) still renders its value for every row. Filter path: every filter is serialized to every user (UI leak) AND TableQueryBuilder::applyFilters (80-87) calls applyToQuery() for every filter unconditionally with no isVisibleFor() check and no user context, so an admin-gated filter remains functional for any caller who supplies ?filter[name]=. Tests: ColumnTypesTest.php:226 asserts isVisibleFor() only in isolation on the Column object; no serialize/apply-time assertion exists; Filter::canSee has no test. Table.php:25-27 docblock explicitly (and falsely) promises canSee is honoured at serialize time — a documented contract, not a deferred feature, so this is framework-bug not app-misuse. Same dead-builder-method meta-class as the round-6 form Component::isVisibleFor and round-4 Dashboard::canSee fixes. Severity medium not high: real silent visibility/authorization bypass for a documented security-relevant API, but exploit requires an app author to actually rely on canSee for sensitive table columns/filters; the showcase baseline uses no ->canSee on table columns/filters, so no live leak in the shipped app today.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: `Column::canSee(Closure)` + `Column::isVisibleFor(?Model)` (packages/table/src/Column.php:204-294) and `Filter::canSee(Closure)` + `Filter::isVisibleFor(?Model)` (packages/table/src/Filter.php:81-120) are public, documented predicates (Table.php:25-27 docblock explicitly promises: "Per-record column visibility (canSee) is honoured when the controller serialises the row"). But grep across all non-test table source shows ZERO callers of either `isVisibleFor`: (1) SERIALIZE path — InertiaDataBuilder::buildIndexPayload serializes ALL columns/filters via `serializeMany($table->getColumns())` / `serializeMany($table->getFilters())` (InertiaDataBuilder.php:205-206), and `serializeMany`/`callToArray` (lines 459-516) never call `isVisibleFor`; `Column::toArray()` (Column.php:360-380) emits only the static `hidden` boolean, NOT the per-record `canSee` result, and is computed once table-wide with no record, so the client cannot honor it either. `serializeRecord` (InertiaDataBuilder.php:530-557) dumps the whole `$record->toArray()` and never filters cells by `Column::isVisibleFor($record)`. (2) APPLY path — TableQueryBuilder::applyFilters (TableQueryBuilder.php:73-88) calls `$filter->applyToQuery()` for every filter unconditionally, never checking `isVisibleFor()`. Net effect: a column declared `->canSee(fn($r)=>$user->canSeeSalary($r))` still renders its value for every row; a filter declared `->canSee(fn()=>$user->isAdmin())` is still serialized to every user AND still applied if `?filter[name]=...` is supplied — a silent visibility/authorization bypass. Tests assert `Column.canSee` only in isolation (ColumnTypesTest.php:226-237); no test asserts serialize-time or apply-time consultation, and Filter::canSee has no test at all. This is the documented-builder-method-never-consumed-at-the-real-entry-point meta-class, distinct from the round-6 form-layout Component::isVisibleFor fix (form package) and round-4 Dashboard::canSee fix (widgets package) — these are the TABLE Column/Filter entry points, which round-6's report incorrectly asserted were already wired ("unlike Column/Filter/Action/NavigationItem::isVisibleFor which are all wired into their serializers").
